page: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 256 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps. The virtualization layer is managed via KVM.

In benchmark evaluations, the b3-256 demonstrates top-tier performance. Multi-core CPU execution via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests rank in the top 10% of tested systems. The server also excels in memory-intensive tasks, with small-block read-only memory bandwidth placing in the top 10%, while large-block read and write operations remain strong in the top 25%. Computational workloads such as software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also achieve top-tier results. For database operations, the instance records top-tier performance in Redis SET operations and strong results in PassMark database benchmarks.

Given its dedicated 64-core architecture without hyperthreading, the b3-256 offers high resource density and predictable compute performance, making it highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring sustained CPU utilization. The absence of shared-core overhead ensures stable execution. This profile is highly suitable for multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, database hosting, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: The OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor featuring 64 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.3 GHz, it is configured with 256 GB of RAM and a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD. Running on a KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers 16 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, Redis SET operations, and software compilation, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database processing capabilities. This balanced resource allocation and dedicated CPU model make the b3-256 highly cost-efficient for sustained, compute-heavy workloads. It is well-suited for database hosting, software compilation, static web serving, and parallel processing tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 256 GB of RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD on a KVM hypervisor.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core AMD EPYC server with NVMe storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and balanced memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with no hyperthreading
256 GB RAM and 400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, Redis, and software compilation benchmarks
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Optimized for database hosting, web serving, and parallel computing
